passage_title: Silbo Gomero – the whistle 'language' of the Canary Islands

sentences.sentence: La Gomera is one of the Canary Islands situated in the Atlantic Ocean off the northwest coast of Africa.
sentences.translation: 拉戈梅拉岛是加那利群岛之一，位于非洲西北海岸外的大西洋中。

structure.text: is one of the Canary Islands
situated in the Atlantic Ocean off the northwest coast of Africa

difficult_points: one of the Canary Islands 表示拉戈梅拉岛属于加那利群岛的一部分。
situated in... 是过去分词短语，修饰 the Canary Islands 或具体说明该岛位置。
off the northwest coast of Africa 表示“离非洲西北海岸不远的海上”。

sentences.sentence: This small volcanic island is mountainous, with steep rocky slopes and deep, wooded ravines, rising to 1,487 metres at its highest peak.
sentences.translation: 这座小型火山岛多山，拥有陡峭的岩石坡面和深而多林的峡谷，最高峰海拔 1,487 米。

structure.text: with steep rocky slopes and deep, wooded ravines
rising to 1,487 metres at its highest peak

difficult_points: with... 短语补充说明岛上的地貌特征。
rising to... 是现在分词短语，说明该岛最高处达到的高度。
its highest peak 中 its 指代 This small volcanic island。

sentences.sentence: It is also home to the best known of the world's whistle 'languages', a means of transmitting information over long distances which is perfectly adapted to the extreme terrain of the island.
sentences.translation: 它也是世界上最著名的哨语所在地；这种方式可以远距离传递信息，并且完美适应了岛上极端的地形。

structure.text: is also home to the best known of the world's whistle 'languages'
a means of transmitting information over long distances
which is perfectly adapted to the extreme terrain of the island

difficult_points: It 指代 La Gomera，而不是 Canary Islands。
a means of... 是对 whistle 'languages' 的同位解释，说明这种“语言”的功能。
which 引导定语从句，修饰 a means，说明其适应岛屿地形。

sentences.sentence: This 'language', known as 'Silbo' or 'Silbo Gomero' – from the Spanish word for 'whistle' – is now shedding light on the language-processing abilities of the human brain, according to scientists.
sentences.translation: 据科学家称，这种“语言”被称为“Silbo”或“Silbo Gomero”——源自西班牙语中“哨声”一词——如今正在揭示人脑处理语言的能力。

structure.text: is now shedding light on the language-processing abilities of the human brain

difficult_points: known as... 是插入的过去分词短语，说明这种语言的名称。
两个破折号之间的 from... 进一步解释名称来源。
shedding light on 是比喻表达，表示“帮助人们理解”。

sentences.sentence: Researchers say that Silbo activates parts of the brain normally associated with spoken language, suggesting that the brain is remarkably flexible in its ability to interpret sounds as language.
sentences.translation: 研究人员表示，Silbo 会激活通常与口语相关的大脑区域，这表明大脑在将声音解读为语言的能力上具有惊人的灵活性。

structure.text: that Silbo activates parts of the brain normally associated with spoken language
suggesting that the brain is remarkably flexible in its ability to interpret sounds as language

difficult_points: that 引导 say 的宾语从句，说明研究发现。
normally associated with spoken language 是过去分词短语，修饰 parts of the brain。
suggesting... 表示前面发现所暗示的结论。
interpret sounds as language 表示把声音当作语言来理解。

sentences.sentence: 'Science has developed the idea of brain areas that are dedicated to language, and we are starting to understand the scope of signals that can be recognised as language,' says David Corina, co-author of a recent study and associate professor of psychology at the University of Washington in Seattle.
sentences.translation: “科学界已经形成了某些大脑区域专用于语言的观念，而我们正开始理解哪些范围内的信号可以被识别为语言，”最近一项研究的合著者、西雅图华盛顿大学心理学副教授大卫·科里纳说。

structure.text: has developed the idea of brain areas
and we are starting to understand the scope of signals
that can be recognised as language
says David Corina, co-author of a recent study and associate professor of psychology at the University of Washington in Seattle

difficult_points: 第一个 that 修饰 brain areas，说明这些区域专用于语言。
and 连接两个并列分句，第二个分句转向研究正在扩展的认识。
第二个 that 修饰 signals，说明哪些信号可被认作语言。
co-author... and associate professor... 是 David Corina 的同位身份说明。

sentences.sentence: Silbo is a substitute for Spanish, with individual words recoded into whistles which have high- and low-frequency tones.
sentences.translation: Silbo 是西班牙语的一种替代形式，其中单个词语被重新编码为带有高低频音调的哨声。

structure.text: with individual words recoded into whistles
which have high- and low-frequency tones

difficult_points: a substitute for Spanish 表示 Silbo 不是独立语法系统，而是西班牙语的替代表达。
with individual words recoded into whistles 说明替代方式是把词语转码为哨声。
which 修饰 whistles，说明哨声包含高低频音调。

sentences.sentence: A whistler – or silbador – puts a finger in his or her mouth to increase the whistle's pitch, while the other hand can be cupped to adjust the direction of the sound.
sentences.translation: 吹哨者——也叫 silbador——会把一根手指放入口中来提高哨声的音高，同时另一只手可以拢成杯状来调整声音的方向。

structure.text: puts a finger in his or her mouth
while the other hand can be cupped to adjust the direction of the sound

difficult_points: 破折号中的 or silbador 是对 A whistler 的另一种称呼。
to increase... 表示把手指放入口中的目的。
while 引导伴随或对比状语从句，说明另一只手同时承担的功能。
can be cupped 是被动结构，指手可以被拢成杯状。

sentences.sentence: 'There is much more ambiguity in the whistled signal than in the spoken signal,' explains lead researcher Manuel Carreiras, psychology professor at the University of La Laguna on the Canary island of Tenerife.
sentences.translation: “哨声信号中的歧义比口语信号中多得多，”首席研究员、特内里费岛拉拉古纳大学心理学教授曼努埃尔·卡雷拉斯解释说。

structure.text: 'There is much more ambiguity in the whistled signal than in the spoken signal
psychology professor at the University of La Laguna on the Canary island of Tenerife

difficult_points: There is... 是存在句结构，说明哨声信号中存在更多歧义。
much more... than... 构成比较结构，对比 whistled signal 和 spoken signal。
psychology professor... 是 Manuel Carreiras 的同位语，补充身份。

sentences.sentence: Because whistled 'words' can be hard to distinguish, silbadores rely on repetition, as well as awareness of context, to make themselves understood.
sentences.translation: 由于哨出来的“词语”可能难以区分，silbadores 依靠重复以及对语境的意识来让别人理解自己。

structure.text: Because whistled 'words' can be hard to distinguish
rely on repetition, as well as awareness of context

difficult_points: Because 从句解释为什么吹哨者需要重复和语境。
as well as awareness of context 补充 rely on 的第二个宾语。
make themselves understood 是 make + 宾语 + 过去分词结构，表示让自己被理解。

sentences.sentence: The silbadores of Gomera are traditionally shepherds and other isolated mountain folk, and their novel means of staying in touch allows them to communicate over distances of up to 10 kilometres.
sentences.translation: 戈梅拉岛的 silbadores 传统上是牧羊人和其他与外界隔绝的山地居民，而他们这种新奇的保持联系方式使他们能够在远达 10 公里的距离上交流。

structure.text: are traditionally shepherds and other isolated mountain folk
and their novel means of staying in touch
allows them to communicate over distances of up to 10 kilometres

difficult_points: and 连接两个并列分句，主语从 silbadores 转为 their novel means。
means of staying in touch 表示保持联系的方式，means 在这里单复数同形。
allows them to communicate 是 allow + 宾语 + 不定式结构。

sentences.sentence: Carreiras explains that silbadores are able to pass a surprising amount of information via their whistles.
sentences.translation: 卡雷拉斯解释说，silbadores 能够通过哨声传递数量惊人的信息。

structure.text: that silbadores are able to pass a surprising amount of information via their whistles

difficult_points: that 引导 explains 的宾语从句，说明解释的具体内容。
are able to 强调吹哨者具备传递大量信息的能力。
via their whistles 表示传递信息的媒介。

sentences.sentence: 'In daily life they use whistles to communicate short commands, but any Spanish sentence could be whistled.'
sentences.translation: “在日常生活中，他们用哨声传达简短命令，但任何西班牙语句子都可以被吹哨表达。”

structure.text: use whistles to communicate short commands
but any Spanish sentence could be whistled

difficult_points: In daily life 限定前半句的实际使用场景。
to communicate short commands 表示使用哨声的目的。
could be whistled 是被动结构，说明任何西班牙语句子理论上都可转为哨声。

sentences.sentence: Silbo has proved particularly useful when fires have occurred on the island and rapid communication across large areas has been vital.
sentences.translation: 当岛上发生火灾、跨大范围的快速沟通至关重要时，Silbo 已被证明尤其有用。

structure.text: when fires have occurred on the island and rapid communication across large areas has been vital

difficult_points: has proved 表示从过去到现在已显示出这种价值。
when 引导时间状语从句，说明 Silbo 特别有用的情境。
fires have occurred 和 communication has been vital 是 when 从句中的并列内容。

sentences.sentence: The study team used neuroimaging equipment to contrast the brain activity of silbadores while listening to whistled and spoken Spanish.
sentences.translation: 研究团队使用神经影像设备，对比 silbadores 在听哨声西班牙语和口语西班牙语时的大脑活动。

structure.text: to contrast the brain activity of silbadores
while listening to whistled and spoken Spanish

difficult_points: to contrast... 表示使用设备的研究目的。
while listening... 是省略结构，逻辑主语是 silbadores。
whistled and spoken Spanish 对比同一种语言的两种输入形式。

sentences.sentence: Results showed the left temporal lobe of the brain, which is usually associated with spoken language, was engaged during the processing of Silbo.
sentences.translation: 结果显示，大脑左颞叶通常与口语相关，而它在处理 Silbo 时被调动了起来。

structure.text: the left temporal lobe of the brain
which is usually associated with spoken language
was engaged during the processing of Silbo

difficult_points: showed 后省略 that，后面是结果内容从句。
which is usually associated with spoken language 是非限制性定语从句，补充说明左颞叶通常功能。
was engaged 是被动结构，表示该脑区在处理 Silbo 时被激活或参与。

sentences.sentence: The researchers found that other key regions in the brain's frontal lobe also responded to the whistles, including those activated in response to sign language among deaf people.
sentences.translation: 研究人员发现，大脑额叶中的其他关键区域也会对这些哨声作出反应，包括那些在聋人对手语作出反应时被激活的区域。

structure.text: that other key regions in the brain's frontal lobe also responded to the whistles
including those activated in response to sign language among deaf people
activated in response to sign language among deaf people

difficult_points: that 引导 found 的宾语从句，说明额叶区域也有反应。
including those... 补充说明 key regions 中包含哪些区域。
those 指代 regions，而不是 researchers。
in response to 表示脑区被激活的触发条件。

sentences.sentence: When the experiments were repeated with non-whistlers, however, activation was observed in all areas of the brain.
sentences.translation: 然而，当这些实验在不会哨语的人身上重复进行时，大脑所有区域都观察到了激活。

structure.text: When the experiments were repeated with non-whistlers
was observed in all areas of the brain

difficult_points: When 从句说明实验重复的对象和情境。
however 表示结果与前文 silbadores 的特定语言区反应形成对比。
was observed 是被动结构，强调观察到的激活现象。

sentences.sentence: 'Our results provide more evidence about the flexibility of human capacity for language in a variety of forms,' Corina says.
sentences.translation: “我们的结果为人类以多种形式使用语言的能力具有灵活性提供了更多证据，”科里纳说。

structure.text: about the flexibility of human capacity for language in a variety of forms

difficult_points: provide more evidence about... 表示研究结果支持某一观点。
human capacity for language 指人类语言能力，而不是某种具体语言。
in a variety of forms 修饰 language，强调语言可有多种形式。

sentences.sentence: 'These data suggest that left-hemisphere language regions are uniquely adapted for communicative purposes, independent of the modality of signal.
sentences.translation: “这些数据表明，左半球语言区域是为交流目的而独特适应的，并且不依赖于信号的形式。

structure.text: that left-hemisphere language regions are uniquely adapted for communicative purposes
independent of the modality of signal

difficult_points: that 引导 suggest 的宾语从句，概括研究数据说明的结论。
left-hemisphere language regions 指大脑左半球中的语言区域。
independent of... 表示这种适应不受信号模式限制。

sentences.sentence: The non-Silbo speakers were not recognising Silbo as a language.
sentences.translation: 不会 Silbo 的人并没有把 Silbo 识别为一种语言。

structure.text: were not recognising Silbo as a language

difficult_points: non-Silbo speakers 指不懂 Silbo 的参与者。
recognising Silbo as a language 使用 recognise ... as ... 结构，表示把 Silbo 当成语言来识别。
were not recognising 强调实验过程中他们没有进行这种语言识别。

sentences.sentence: They had nothing to grab onto, so multiple areas of their brains were activated.'
sentences.translation: 他们没有任何可以抓住的线索，因此他们大脑的多个区域都被激活了。”

structure.text: so multiple areas of their brains were activated

difficult_points: They 指代 non-Silbo speakers。
nothing to grab onto 是比喻表达，指没有可用于理解的语言线索。
so 引出结果，说明缺乏线索导致大脑多区域泛化激活。

sentences.sentence: Carreiras says the origins of Silbo Gomero remain obscure, but that indigenous Canary Islanders, who were of North African origin, already had a whistled language when Spain conquered the volcanic islands in the 15th century.
sentences.translation: 卡雷拉斯说，Silbo Gomero 的起源仍不清楚，但具有北非血统的加那利群岛原住民在 15 世纪西班牙征服这些火山岛时已经拥有一种哨语。

structure.text: the origins of Silbo Gomero remain obscure
but that indigenous Canary Islanders, who were of North African origin, already had a whistled language
who were of North African origin
when Spain conquered the volcanic islands in the 15th century

difficult_points: says 后有两个并列宾语内容：起源不明，以及原住民已有哨语。
but that... 中 that 与前面省略的 that 并列，保持内容从句结构。
who were of North African origin 是非限制性定语从句，补充说明 indigenous Canary Islanders。
when 引导时间状语从句，说明已有哨语发生在西班牙征服时。

sentences.sentence: Whistled languages survive today in Papua New Guinea, Mexico, Vietnam, Guyana, China, Nepal, Senegal, and a few mountainous pockets in southern Europe.
sentences.translation: 如今，哨语仍存在于巴布亚新几内亚、墨西哥、越南、圭亚那、中国、尼泊尔、塞内加尔以及南欧少数山区小片地区。

structure.text: in Papua New Guinea, Mexico, Vietnam, Guyana, China, Nepal, Senegal, and a few mountainous pockets in southern Europe

difficult_points: survive today 表示这种语言形式至今仍保留，而不是指人或动物生存。
in 后面列举多个地区，说明哨语并非只存在于加那利群岛。
mountainous pockets 指分散的小型山区区域。

sentences.sentence: There are thought to be as many as 70 whistled languages still in use, though only 12 have been described and studied scientifically.
sentences.translation: 人们认为仍在使用的哨语多达 70 种，尽管只有 12 种得到过科学描述和研究。

structure.text: are thought to be as many as 70 whistled languages still in use
though only 12 have been described and studied scientifically

difficult_points: There are thought to be 表示“被认为有”，是被动估计表达。
as many as 70 强调数量之多。
though 引导让步状语从句，对比存在数量和被科学研究的数量。

sentences.sentence: This form of communication is an adaptation found among cultures where people are often isolated from each other, according to Julien Meyer, a researcher at the Institute of Human Sciences in Lyon, France.
sentences.translation: 据法国里昂人文科学研究所研究员朱利安·迈耶所说，这种交流形式是一种适应性变化，存在于人们常彼此隔绝的文化之中。

structure.text: is an adaptation found among cultures
where people are often isolated from each other
a researcher at the Institute of Human Sciences in Lyon, France

difficult_points: This form of communication 指哨语这种交流形式。
found among cultures 是过去分词短语，修饰 adaptation。
where 引导定语从句，修饰 cultures，说明这些文化的共同环境。
a researcher... 是 Julien Meyer 的同位语。

sentences.sentence: 'They are mostly used in mountains or dense forests,' he says.
sentences.translation: “它们大多用于山区或茂密森林中，”他说。

structure.text: are mostly used in mountains or dense forests

difficult_points: They 指代前文的 whistled languages。
are mostly used 是被动结构，表示主要使用场景。
mountains or dense forests 对应人群隔绝、远距离沟通困难的环境。

sentences.sentence: 'Whistled languages are quite clearly defined and represent an original adaptation of the spoken language for the needs of isolated human groups.'
sentences.translation: “哨语的定义相当明确，并代表了口语为满足隔绝人群需要而形成的一种原创性适应。”

structure.text: and represent an original adaptation of the spoken language
for the needs of isolated human groups

difficult_points: are quite clearly defined 和 represent... 是并列谓语。
adaptation of the spoken language 表示哨语是口语的一种适应性变体。
for the needs of... 表示这种适应服务于隔绝人群的需求。

sentences.sentence: But with modern communication technology now widely available, researchers say whistled languages like Silbo are threatened with extinction.
sentences.translation: 但是，随着现代通信技术如今已广泛可用，研究人员表示，像 Silbo 这样的哨语正面临灭绝威胁。

structure.text: But with modern communication technology now widely available
whistled languages like Silbo are threatened with extinction

difficult_points: But 表示从哨语的价值转向其生存危机。
with modern communication technology now widely available 是原因或背景状语。
are threatened with extinction 是被动结构，说明哨语面临消失风险。

sentences.sentence: With dwindling numbers of Gomera islanders still fluent in the language, Canaries' authorities are taking steps to try to ensure its survival.
sentences.translation: 随着仍能流利使用这种语言的戈梅拉岛居民人数不断减少，加那利群岛当局正在采取措施，努力确保它的存续。

structure.text: With dwindling numbers of Gomera islanders still fluent in the language
to try to ensure its survival

difficult_points: With dwindling numbers... 说明当局采取行动的背景。
still fluent in the language 是形容词短语，修饰 Gomera islanders。
to try to ensure its survival 表示采取措施的目的，its 指 Silbo。

sentences.sentence: Since 1999, Silbo Gomero has been taught in all of the island's elementary schools.
sentences.translation: 自 1999 年以来，Silbo Gomero 已经在岛上所有小学中教授。

structure.text: has been taught in all of the island's elementary schools

difficult_points: Since 1999 与现在完成时 has been taught 搭配，表示从过去持续到现在。
has been taught 是被动结构，强调 Silbo Gomero 被纳入学校教学。
all of the island's elementary schools 表示覆盖岛上所有小学。

sentences.sentence: In addition, locals are seeking assistance from the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO).
sentences.translation: 此外，当地人正在向联合国教育、科学及文化组织（UNESCO）寻求帮助。

structure.text: are seeking assistance from the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO)

difficult_points: In addition 表示在学校教学之外还有另一项保护行动。
are seeking assistance 使用现在进行时，说明行动正在进行。
from 后面是组织全称，括号中的 UNESCO 是其缩写。

sentences.sentence: 'The local authorities are trying to get an award from the organisation to declare [Silbo Gomero] as something that should be preserved for humanity,' Carreiras adds.
sentences.translation: “地方当局正努力从该组织获得一项认定，把 [Silbo Gomero] 宣布为应为人类保存的事物，”卡雷拉斯补充道。

structure.text: are trying to get an award from the organisation
that should be preserved for humanity

difficult_points: are trying to get... 表示当局正在争取某种认可或奖项。
to declare... 表示获得该 award 的目的。
that should be preserved for humanity 修饰 something，说明 Silbo Gomero 的保护价值。
[Silbo Gomero] 方括号表示原文引语中为了清晰而补入的对象。
